name: Toyol Servant
description: The Toyol is a mischievous undead infant spirit in Malay folklore. It is typically created through black magic rituals, often involving the corpse of a stillborn or aborted fetus. The Toyol is bound to its master and serves as a servant, primarily used to steal money or valuables. It is said to be childlike in nature and easily distracted by toys or small objects. While powerful in its own right, a Toyol lacks the intelligence to act independently and requires constant direction from its master. The rituals to create and control a Toyol are complex and dangerous, often involving grave robbing and the manipulation of dark energies. The Toyol is a symbol of forbidden knowledge and the consequences of tampering with the natural order. Its existence serves as a cautionary tale against greed and the pursuit of wealth through unethical means. Possessing a Toyol is considered highly taboo and carries significant spiritual risk. A Toyol's presence can bring misfortune to a household if not properly controlled and appeased.
subtitle: Undead infant spirit of servitude
appearance: The Toyol appears as a small, naked child, often with grayish or greenish skin. Its eyes are usually large and black, and its teeth are sharp. It may have a bald head or sparse, thin hair. Some descriptions depict it as having a deformed or unnatural appearance, reflecting its unnatural creation. It is typically depicted as small and agile, capable of moving quickly and silently.
habitat: Toyols are typically kept in jars or other containers when not actively serving their master. They are often found in homes, shops, or other places where their master seeks to steal from. They can also be found near graveyards or other places associated with death and dark magic.
behavior: Toyols are mischievous and obedient servants, but they require constant supervision. They are easily distracted by toys or shiny objects and may wander off if not properly controlled. They are driven by a childlike desire for attention and affection, but their undead nature makes them incapable of genuine emotion. They are fiercely loyal to their master and will carry out their commands without question, regardless of the consequences.

keyMyths.title: The Creation Ritual
keyMyths.description: The Toyol is created through a dark magic ritual involving the corpse of a stillborn or aborted fetus. The ritual is complex and dangerous, requiring specific ingredients and incantations.

keyMyths.title: The Master's Control
keyMyths.description: A Toyol is bound to its master and must obey their commands. The master typically keeps the Toyol in a jar and feeds it offerings to maintain its loyalty.

sources.author: Rosemary Guiley
sources.work: The Encyclopedia of Demons and Demonology
sources.description: Provides a general overview of demonic creatures and folklore from various cultures.
